en: TO THE CHOIRMASTER. A PSALM OF THE SONS OF KORAH. Hear this, all peoples! Give ear, all inhabitants of the world,
zh: 萬民哪！你們要聽這話； 世上的居民哪！你們要留心聽。

en: both low and high, rich and poor together!
zh: 不論地位高低，或貧或富， 都要一同留心聽。

en: My mouth shall speak wisdom; the meditation of my heart shall be understanding.
zh: 我的口要說出智慧的話； 我的心要默想明智的事。

en: I will incline my ear to a proverb; I will solve my riddle to the music of the lyre.
zh: 我要留心聆聽箴言； 我要彈琴解開謎語。

en: Why should I fear in times of trouble, when the iniquity of those who cheat me surrounds me,
zh: 在患難的日子，暗中迫害我的惡人圍繞我的時候， 我何須懼怕呢？

en: those who trust in their wealth and boast of the abundance of their riches?
zh: 他們倚靠財富， 自誇多財。

en: Truly no man can ransom another, or give to God the price of his life,
zh: 但他們沒有一個能把他的兄弟贖回， 或把他的贖價交給 神，

en: for the ransom of their life is costly and can never suffice,
that he should live on forever and never see the pit.
zh: 使他永遠活著， 不見朽壞。

en: For he sees that even the wise die; the fool and the stupid alike must perish and leave their wealth to others.
zh: 他看見智慧人死去， 愚昧人和愚頑人一同滅亡， 把他們的財產留給別人。

en: Their graves are their homes forever, their dwelling places to all generations, though they called lands by their own names.
zh: 他們心裡想，他們的家必永存， 他們的住處必留到萬代； 他們以自己的名，稱自己的地方。

en: Man in his pomp will not remain; he is like the beasts that perish.
zh: 但是人不能長享富貴； 他們就像要滅亡的牲畜一樣。

en: This is the path of those who have foolish confidence; yet after them people approve of their boasts. Selah
zh: 這就是愚昧人的道路； 但在他們以後的人，還欣賞他們的話。

en: Like sheep they are appointed for Sheol; Death shall be their shepherd, and the upright shall rule over them in the morning. Their form shall be consumed in Sheol, with no place to dwell.
zh: 他們好像羊群被派定下陰間； 死亡必作他們的牧人。 到了早晨，正直人要管轄他們； 他們的形體必被陰間消滅， 他們再沒有住處。

en: But God will ransom my soul from the power of Sheol, for he will receive me. Selah
zh: 但 神必救贖我的靈魂脫離陰間的權勢， 因為他必把我接去。

en: Be not afraid when a man becomes rich, when the glory of his house increases.
zh: 別人發財，家室增榮的時候， 你不要懼怕。

en: For when he dies he will carry nothing away; his glory will not go down after him.
zh: 因為他死的時候，甚麼也不能帶走； 他的榮華也不能隨他下去。

en: For though, while he lives, he counts himself blessed,- and though you get praise when you do well for yourself-
zh: 他在世的時候，雖然自稱是有福的，並且說： “只要你豐足，人就稱讚你。”

en: his soul will go to the generation of his fathers, who will never again see light.
zh: 他還要歸到他歷代的祖宗那裡去， 永不再見光明。

en: Man in his pomp yet without understanding is like the beasts that perish.
zh: 人享富貴而不聰明， 就像要滅亡的牲畜一樣。
